TSLA: Elon Musk Says He Hasn't Sold Tesla Stock For Over Three Years: 'Bought A Billion Dollars...' - Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA). Tesla CEO Elon Musk stated he has not sold any Tesla stock for over three years, confirming he bought a billion dollars worth of shares last year. This revelation comes as Tesla unveiled a new lithium refinery in the U.S. and amid ongoing discussions about the EV sector and Musk's other ventures like SpaceX.
TGT: Anti-ICE protesters stage sit-in at Twin Cities Target store. Anti-ICE protesters staged a sit-in at a West St. Paul Target store, singing songs and chanting slogans to urge Target to condemn U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement operations in Minnesota. The protest is part of a larger movement asking Target to bar ICE from using its properties and to address the arrests of its employees by ICE. Similar protests, including one involving faith leaders at Target's headquarters and a "salt melting ice" campaign, have been ongoing, criticizing Target's silence on the federal immigration crackdown.

Target Corporation (TGT) Boosts Investment to $5 Billion, Reshaping Retail Model. Target Corporation is increasing its capital spending to $5 billion in fiscal 2026 to enhance operational efficiency and market competitiveness. This investment will focus on technology upgrades, including machine learning and AI for improved forecasting, and a significant store layout transformation to uplift customer experience. Additionally, the company has reduced 1,800 headquarters roles to streamline decision-making, aiming to restore discretionary momentum and tighten merchandising discipline.

Target faces renewed backlash after Border Patrol detentions at Minnesota store (TGT:NYSE). Target (TGT) is facing renewed backlash and boycott calls after federal immigration agents detained two employees outside one of its stores in Richfield, Minnesota. The incident, captured on video, has ignited protests and added to Target's history of political controversies. This event raises concerns about potential impacts on shopper sentiment, customer loyalty, sales, and Target's reputation, echoing risks seen in past controversies.

QRG Capital Management Inc. Has $5.15 Million Stock Holdings in Target Corporation $TGT. QRG Capital Management Inc. decreased its stake in Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) by 27.8% in Q3, selling 22,084 shares and retaining 57,372 shares valued at $5.146 million. Target exceeded quarterly EPS expectations at $1.78 but saw revenue decline by 1.6% year-over-year to $25.27 billion. Analysts currently rate Target as a "Hold" with a consensus target price of $103.67, and institutional investors own approximately 79.73% of the company's stock.
SBUX: Allstate Corp Acquires Shares of 41,263 Starbucks Corporation $SBUX. Allstate Corp has acquired a new position of 41,263 shares in Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ:SBUX) during the third quarter, valued at approximately $3.49 million. This comes amidst other institutional investor activity and an insider share acquisition by Director Jorgen Vig Knudstorp. The article also details Starbucks' recent financial performance, dividend announcement, and current analyst ratings.

Kroger's $38M Crawfordsville store to feature sushi, Starbucks. Kroger is investing $38 million to build a new, larger format Marketplace store and fuel center in Crawfordsville. This new store, expected to open in a year, will offer expanded features such as Murray's Cheese, Starbucks, fresh sushi, and apparel. The project is part of a broader $200 million investment by Kroger's Central Division for new constructions and remodels.
DIS: Csenge Advisory Group Sells 8,526 Shares of The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Csenge Advisory Group reduced its stake in The Walt Disney Company (DIS) by 54.3% in the third quarter, selling 8,526 shares and retaining 7,163 shares valued at approximately $820,000. Despite missing revenue estimates, Disney beat EPS expectations and declared a $0.75 dividend. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus with a target price of $135.20, while the stock currently trades around $111.
NKE: London Spirit kicks off new ownership era with Barclays, Nike deals. London Spirit, a franchise in English cricket's The Hundred competition, has secured major sponsorship deals with Barclays Bank as its principal partner and Nike as its new kit supplier. These agreements follow the sale of a 49% stake in the team to the US consortium Tech Titans last year, which has allowed the team to independently manage its sponsorship assets. The new partnerships and updated branding signal a new commercial era for London Spirit.
